population: Sepsis patients from a large multicentre cohort
cohort_gender: Male: N=4622; Female: N=4792 (out of N=9414)
notes: Study objective stated; no actual statistical results or confirmed findings are present in the provided excerpt.

cohort_gender: Male: N=4622; Female: N=4792 (out of N=9414)
timing: Within initial 24 hours of ICU admission
method: Multivariate regression, smoothed curve fitting, threshold effects analysis
notes: Patients classified into quartiles based on MCV levels. Adjustments made for age, sex, BMI, vital signs, and comorbidities. Data from eICU Collaborative Research Database 2014-2015. Retrospective cohort study.

cohort_gender: Male: N=4622; Female: N=4792 (out of N=9414)
method: Multivariable logistic regression with smoothed fitted curves (nonlinear analysis)
effect_size: OR 1.11 (95% CI 1.01, 1.23) per 1 fl increase in MCV overall; OR 1.10 (95% CI 1.02, 1.17) per 1 fl increase at MCV <83 fl
performance: P=0.04 (overall); P=0.004 (MCV <83 fl segment); 28-day mortality rate 9.38% (883/9415)
notes: Nonlinear positive correlation between MCV and 28-day mortality. Inflection point at MCV=83 fl. Significant association only confirmed below the inflection point.

cohort_gender: Male: N=4622; Female: N=4792 (out of N=9414)
notes: Data sourced from the eICU Collaborative Research Database. Full statistical results not provided in the excerpt.

cohort_gender: Male: N=4622; Female: N=4792 (out of N=9414)
notes: Non-linear positive correlation between MCV and 28-day mortality risk; clinicians should monitor MCV especially in patients with high MCV levels.

cohort_gender: Male: N=4622; Female: N=4792 (out of N=9414)
predictor: Mean corpuscular volume (MCV) - Q1 vs Q2 vs Q3 vs Q4
method: Logistic regression (unadjusted, Model I adjusted for age and sex, Model II fully adjusted); generalized additive model for non-linear association; log-likelihood ratio test
notes: Three models developed: crude (unadjusted), Model I (adjusted for age and sex), Model II (fully adjusted for confounding variables). Quartiles used as categorical parameters. Non-linear association assessed; threshold effect analysed with smoothed fitted curve. Sensitivity analyses conducted for ICU stay <24h and other subgroups.

population: Sepsis patients divided into quartiles based on MCV values
cohort_gender: Male: N=4622; Female: N=4792 (out of N=9414)
notes: Age increased progressively from Q1 to Q4 (62.15±16.52 to 67.99±14.56 years, p<0.001); BMI decreased from Q1 to Q4 (29.68±10.01 to 28.32±8.49, p<0.001); sex distribution did not differ significantly across quartiles (p=0.128)

cohort_gender: Male: N=4622; Female: N=4792 (out of N=9414)
method: Multivariable logistic regression (Model II, adjusted for all potential confounding variables)
effect_size: OR 1.11 (95% CI 1.01, 1.23) per 1 fl increase; OR 1.50 (95% CI 1.10, 2.05) for Q4 (95.5-138.9 fl) vs reference
notes: Three models presented: unadjusted, Model I, and Model II. Greatest increase in 28-day mortality risk observed in Q4 group in fully adjusted Model II.

source_info.section: **The results of relationship between MCV and 28-day mortality**

cohort_gender: Male: N=4622; Female: N=4792 (out of N=9414)
effect_size: Highest risk RR 1.04 (95% CI: 1.02, 1.05) in age group 15–59 years
performance: Significant interaction with age (P=0.040); moderating effect of body temperature (P=0.050); no significant effect of gender (P=0.105), BMI (P=0.608), respiratory rate (P=0.057), heart rate (P=0.121), MAP (P=0.688)
notes: All factors except gender were significantly associated with 28-day mortality (P<0.05) in univariate analysis

cohort_gender: Male: N=4622; Female: N=4792 (out of N=9414)
method: Restricted cubic spline / dose-response analysis with linear model (Model I) and two-stage non-linear model (Model II), adjusted for multiple covariates
notes: Nonlinear dose-response relationship observed between MCV and 28-day mortality. Covariates adjusted: age, gender, BMI, temperature, respiratory rate, heart rate, MAP, acute physiology score III, APACHE IV score, AIDS, hepatic failure, metastatic cancer, immunosuppression, albumin, lactate, platelets, hemoglobin, RDW, white blood cell count. Full results presented in Table 4 (not provided in excerpt).

population: Sepsis patients admitted to ICU, stratified by MCV quartiles
cohort_gender: Male: N=4622; Female: N=4792 (out of N=9414)
predictor: Mean Corpuscular Volume (MCV) quartiles (Q1: 58.0-86.0 fl, Q2: 86.1-90.9 fl, Q3: 91.0-95.4 fl, Q4: not fully reported)
method: Quartile stratification with baseline characteristics comparison
notes: Table 1 presents baseline characteristics by MCV quartile. Statistically significant differences across quartiles were observed for age (p<0.001), BMI (p<0.001), temperature (p=0.003), respiratory rate (p=0.016), heart rate (p<0.001), MAP (p<0.001), Acute Physiology Score III (p<0.001), APACHE IV score (p<0.001), lactate (p<0.001), MCHC (p<0.001), platelets (p<0.001), hemoglobin (p<0.001), RDW (p<0.001). Gender (p=0.128), white blood cell count (p=0.065), and AIDS (p=0.721) were not significantly different across quartiles. Q4 upper bound not reported in extracted text.

population: ICU sepsis patients stratified by RDW quartiles

cohort_gender: Male: N=4622; Female: N=4792 (out of N=9414)
method: Generalized additive model (GAM) with threshold effect analysis (piecewise regression)
performance: MCV <83 fL: p=0.004; MCV >83 fL: p=0.31; Difference p=0.018; Likelihood ratio test p=0.011
notes: Nonlinear threshold association identified. Knot at MCV=83 fL. Significant positive association between MCV and 28-day mortality only below the threshold of 83 fL. Adjusted for age, gender, BMI, temperature, respiratory rate, heart rate, MAP, Acute Physiology Score III, APACHE IV score, AIDS, hepatic failure, metastatic cancer, immunosuppression, albumin, lactate, platelets, hemoglobin, RDW, and white blood cell count.

cohort_gender: Male: N=4622; Female: N=4792 (out of N=9414)
method: ROC analysis, logistic regression, Kaplan-Meier survival analysis
notes: Study title only provided; no detailed results, tables, or statistical outputs were included in the excerpt.

population: Septic patients admitted to ICU, defined using ICD code A41.9, primarily older adults (mean age 65.7±15.8 years)
cohort_gender: Male: N=4622; Female: N=4792 (out of N=9414)
predictor: Mean Corpuscular Volume (MCV) below threshold of 83 fl
method: Multivariable logistic regression with nonlinear (piecewise) analysis
effect_size: OR 1.10 (95% CI 1.02–1.17) per 1 fl increase below 83 fl threshold
notes: Above 83 fl, no significant association was observed (OR 1.01, 95% CI 0.99–1.02, P=0.31), indicating a nonlinear relationship. 28-day ICU mortality rate in cohort was 9.38%. Data sourced from eICU multicenter critical care database across 208 hospitals in the United States.

cohort_gender: Male: N=4622; Female: N=4792 (out of N=9414)
notes: The study examines the correlation between MCV and mortality in sepsis patients, proposing mechanisms including erythrocyte morphology changes linked to inflammatory markers and oxidative stress impairing erythrocyte deformability. MCV described as potentially providing independent prognostic information. Full results not reported in this excerpt.

cohort_gender: Male: N=4622; Female: N=4792 (out of N=9414)
method: ROC analysis with incremental predictive value assessment after adjusting for lactate
effect_size: MCV > 83 fL identified as clinically relevant threshold
notes: MCV retains significant incremental predictive value after adjusting for lactate. Proposed as supplementary indicator in sepsis risk stratification model. Nonlinear association observed between MCV and 28-day mortality.

cohort_gender: Male: N=4622; Female: N=4792 (out of N=9414)
method: Nonlinear association analysis with threshold detection
effect_size: Below 83 fl threshold: each 1 fl increase in MCV associated with 10% increase in mortality risk (OR or HR ~1.10); above 83 fl: no significant association
performance: Critical threshold at 83 fl; p-value not reported
notes: Multicenter retrospective cohort study. Nonlinear relationship identified with inflection point at MCV = 83 fl. No significant association observed above the threshold.

cohort_gender: Male: N=4622; Female: N=4792 (out of N=9414)
method: ROC analysis, logistic regression, Kaplan-Meier survival analysis
